title: Coral Carbohydrate Assay for 96-well plates
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d9d5u","text":"This protocol is designed to work with coral host tissue slurry that has been processed by airbrushing to remove the tissue from the skeleton and after removal of symbiont cells and homoginization. This protocol will allow quantification of carbohydrate concentrations of samples using 96-well plates for rapid assessment. This protocol was adapted from Masuko et al 20051. For calculation of carbohydrate concentrations, follow the directions presented in the materials section or use the custom Markdown file that can be found on my GitHub (CarbohydrateAssay_96wellplate).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":371,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":543,"length":29}],"data":{}},{"key":"df9mo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"diahm","text":"1. Masuko T, Minami A, Iwasaki N, Majima T, Nishimura S, Lee YC. Carbohydrate analysis by a phenol-sulfuric acid method in microplate format. Anal Biochem. 2005 Apr 1;339(1):69-72. doi: 10.1016/j.ab.2004.12.001. PMID: 15766712.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"06803091BF1F11EBB53D0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://github.com/seabove7/protocols/tree/main/CarbohydrateAssay_96wellplate"}}}}

steps: Pull desired coral slurries from the -80 freezer to thaw at room temperature
Collect test tubes and label ( 10 for the standard + the number of samples running )
Vortex samples after thawing (samples were previously broken up via bead beating with glass beads)
Set up a room temperature water bath in the fume hood with test tube rack (DI water in a plastic bin is fine)
Add 25 uL of phenol to first sample
Immediately add 2.5 mL sulphuric acid to the sample
Incubate the sample at room temperature for 1 minute
When the last sample is placed in the water bath, incubate all samples for 30 minutes

Pipette 200 uL of all standards and samples into the bottom of the wells in a 96-well plate (see the two different plate layouts for either sets of 3 or 4 per sample) %s %s Plate layout for 3 sample replicates123456789101112ABBB888S7S7S7S15S15S15B111999S8S8S8S16S16S16C222S1S1S1S9S9S9S17S17S17D333S2S2S2S10S10S10S18S18S18E444S3S3S3S11S11S11S19S19S19F555S4S4S4S12S12S12S20S20S20G666S5S5S5S13S13S13S21S21S21H777S6S6S6S14S14S14S22S22S22 Plate layout for 4 sample replicates123456789101112ABBB888S4S6S8S10S12S14B111999S4S6S8S10S12S14C222S1S2S3S4S6S8S10S12S14D333S1S2S3S4S6S8S10S12S14E444S1S2S3S5S7S9S11S13S15F555S1S2S3S5S7S9S11S13S15G666S5S7S9S11S13S15H777 S5S7S9S11S13S15
Cover plate and then read on spectrophotometer at 485 nm and then calculate sample concentrations following method listed under 'Materials'
Add 100 uL of coral slurry and 900 uL milliQ water to pre-labelled test tube for all samples* You many need to concentrate or dilute your samples more depending on how they were collected.
creator: Colleen B Bove
